findings.message: The new `verify-plan-artifact-refs` branch only handles `[OK]` and `SpecRef hash mismatch`, but the verifier also emits unresolved anchors, invalid anchors, coverage violations, and I/O/JSON failures. Those outcomes are now undefined, so a non-hash verifier failure can neither be repaired nor failed closed.

The recovery path tells `type-designer` to refresh `<layer>-catalogue-spec-signals.json`, but that agent's own contract restricts Bash to its internal pipeline commands and does not include `catalogue-spec-signals` (`.claude/agents/type-designer.md:472-474`). The requested remediation is therefore outside the subagent's allowed tool scope and may never execute.

`D4` says `files` should go through `ReviewCycle::get_scope_target`, but `D6` says the new scope-routing query must be implemented as a separate application service independent of `ReviewCycle`. Those prescriptions conflict, so the intended dependency shape is unclear.

D4 says `--track-id` is required because `track/review-scope.json` needs `<track-id>` placeholder expansion, but `classify` is specified everywhere as path-only while still using the same scope routing and even displaying `other_track` exclusions. That leaves `sotp review classify track/items/...` under-specified for track-scoped paths: the command cannot deterministically know which track is the current one unless the ADR defines a track-id source for `classify` too.

Rejected Alternative E says the zero-argument `classify` use case, “all current diff paths -> scope mapping,” is already covered by `review results`, but the Context explicitly says `review results` does not output per-scope individual file lists. This makes the rejection rationale internally inconsistent; it should be reframed around `files --scope`/shell composition or another non-contradictory reason.

The `files` command signature is inconsistent: D1 introduces `sotp review files --scope <name>` and D2 repeats that adopted form without `--track-id`, but D4 later makes `--track-id` a required argument for `files` and D5's example includes it. This leaves the CLI contract ambiguous for implementers and users.

T001 says `ScopeQueryInteractor` has only `scope_config` and `base` fields, but the same task also says `files()` calls `diff_getter.list_diff_files(&self.base)`. With no stored `diff_getter` field or equivalent state, this service cannot be implemented as written.

The `files` API is typed as `files(scope: ScopeName)`, but this module also says `UnknownScope` is emitted when the requested scope name is not in `scope_config.all_scope_names()`. Once the CLI has converted user input into `ScopeName`, there is no invalid-scope state left for the service to detect, so this error path is unreachable unless the API carries the raw string or a validation result.

T001 describes `ScopeQueryInteractor` as holding only `scope_config` and `base`, but the same task requires `files()` to call `diff_getter.list_diff_files(&self.base)`. As written, there is no stored `DiffGetter` receiver, so the interactor cannot implement the required method literally. This also conflicts with the generated usecase contract, which models `diff_getter: D` as an owned field.

AC-06 conflicts with the rest of the classify contract: `IN-01`/`AC-01`-`AC-05` and `plan.md` T002 require `classify` to accept at least one path and emit one `<path>\t<scope>` row per input, so empty stdout is not reachable just because no named scope matched. The only empty-output case would be zero inputs, which the CLI forbids.

The `ReviewScopeConfig` contract is internally inconsistent: the prose says `classify(&[FilePath])`, but `expected_methods` records the parameter as `Vec<FilePath>`. That changes the advertised API shape for downstream codegen and makes the domain contract self-contradictory.

`ScopeClassification::Named` is typed as `Vec<ScopeName>`, but `ScopeName` already includes the implicit `Other` variant. As written, the plan allows a "named" classification to carry `Other`, which conflicts with the description that Named holds only named scopes and makes the `Named`/`Other` split ambiguous.

T003 says unknown scopes are validated with `scope_config.all_scope_names()`, but the spec's IN-04 and the `ReviewScopeConfig` contract require `contains_scope` for this check. That gives implementors two different validation rules for the same command, so the plan is spec-inconsistent as written.

`task-coverage.json` marks AC-11 as covered by T001/T002/T003, but AC-11 also requires the final `cargo make check-layers` gate to pass. Only T004 actually runs that check, so this coverage entry overstates completion of the criterion and can report the layer-boundary check as satisfied before it has been executed.

T002 makes `sotp review classify` resolve the diff base and wire `GitDiffGetter`/`base` into the interactor even though the command never consumes diff-derived data. That adds an unrelated failure mode to a path-only classifier, so a valid repo-relative path can still fail before classification if base resolution is unavailable.

S1/T001 asks `ScopeQueryInteractor::classify` to emit `ScopeClassification::Excluded`, but the only documented domain input is `ReviewScopeConfig::classify(&[FilePath]) -> HashMap<ScopeName, Vec<FilePath>>` plus `contains_scope`/`all_scope_names`. That model can distinguish `Main` vs `Other`, but it has no way to tell whether a path missing from the map is `Other` or `<excluded>`, so the interactor cannot implement AC-03/AC-10 as written. The domain contract needs an explicit exclusion signal or the usecase type needs to drop `Excluded`.

AC-02 specifies comma-separated output for multi-match paths, but the plan never defines a canonical ordering for the matched scope names. Because `ReviewScopeConfig::classify` is backed by hash maps, `Named(Vec<MainScopeName>)` can be rendered in different orders across runs unless the plan adds an explicit sort, making the CLI output nondeterministic and the acceptance criterion flaky.

`ScopeClassification::Named(Vec<MainScopeName>)` is described as representing "one or more" matches, but the chosen type still admits `Named([])`. That means the plan does not actually make the stated invariant unrepresentable, so the contract is weaker than the text claims.

`ScopeQueryInteractor`'s contract describes classifying named matches as `Named(Vec<MainScopeName>)`, but the same usecase catalogue defines `ScopeClassification` as `Named(MainScopeName, Vec<MainScopeName>)` to make the non-empty invariant unrepresentable. Since the rendered markdown only lists the variant name, this conflicting JSON prose can drive an implementation toward a possibly-empty `Vec`, undermining the intended type invariant.

IN-08 states that both `classify` and `files` output scope names constrained by `scope_config.all_scope_names()`, but the same spec defines `files` as outputting only file paths in IN-02/IN-06/AC-07. This makes the `files` contract self-contradictory; either limit this universe rule to `classify` output, or reword it as validation of the requested `--scope` for `files`.

The interactor description says `files` calls `diff_getter.list_diff_files` and then classifies, while the error contract says the service checks `contains_scope` and returns `UnknownScope` for an undefined scope. If diff retrieval happens first, an undefined scope can surface `DiffGet` instead of `UnknownScope`, contradicting the service error semantics and the planned UnknownScope test. The plan should require scope validation before any diff access in `ScopeQueryInteractor::files`.

`ScopeQueryService` describes `ScopeClassification` as one of the domain types it depends on, but the same contract defines `ScopeClassification` as a new usecase enum. That is a semantic layer-placement error in the type contract and conflicts with the plan's usecase-layer service design.

The implementation plan gives conflicting behavior for `ScopeQueryInteractor::files`: this task text says `files` gets diff files via `diff_getter.list_diff_files` and then classifies them, while the usecase type contract requires `files` to validate `scope_config.contains_scope` first and return `UnknownScope` before any diff I/O. If implemented from this plan text, an unknown scope can surface a diff/base failure instead of the AC-08 `Unknown scope` error.

T003 wires `files` through `build_v2_shared` and only then validates `scope_config.contains_scope()`. `build_v2_shared` resolves the diff base first, so an invalid `--scope` can still fail on unrelated `.commit_hash`/`git rev-parse main` errors before the planned `Unknown scope: ...` path is reachable. That makes AC-08 order-dependent on diff-base state and contradicts the validation-first flow described in the same section.

`ScopeClassification::Named` is specified in terms of `MainScopeName`, and `ScopeName` is also described as `Main(MainScopeName)`, but the type catalogue never introduces `MainScopeName` as a standalone contract type. That leaves the new `Named` payload and the unknown-scope path underspecified at the artifact level, so the usecase contract cannot be implemented from the published plan alone.

`usecase-types.md` still leaves two T001-owned entries in mixed states: `PathClassification` is `Signal=🔵 / Cat-Spec=🟡` and `ScopeQueryInteractor` is `Signal=🟡 / Cat-Spec=🔵`. That conflicts with the same artifact set marking T001 as `done` in `impl-plan.json` / `plan.md`, so the generated plan artifacts are internally inconsistent about whether the completed task has fully resolved its usecase types.

T001 only calls out `ScopeQueryInteractor::files` tests for `UnknownScope` and `DiffGet` errors. The successful path of the public `files` API is missing from the plan, so a regression in the `contains_scope`/`classify` wiring could ship without any direct coverage.

T003 promises human-readable handling for `DiffGet` failures, but the listed unit tests never exercise that branch. Add an explicit failure-path test for the CLI so the stderr/exit-status behavior cannot regress silently.

T001 is marked `done`, but the same change also alters the completed task's definition by adding a new success-path unit test requirement. A completion-only transition should not expand the scope of the task being marked complete, because the `done` status now applies to a materially different task definition than the one that was pending before this change.

T003 is still `todo`, but this plan-artifact update also changes its acceptance/test requirements by adding DiffGet error-path coverage. That is unrelated to the T001 completion transition and contradicts the stated plan-artifact intent that T002-T004 remain unchanged in this round.

`classify` builds the scope list by inverting `ReviewScopeConfig::classify` into a `HashMap<FilePath, Vec<MainScopeName>>`, but it does not deduplicate scope names per path. Because the public API preserves one output per input path, a duplicated input like `["libs/domain/src/lib.rs", "libs/domain/src/lib.rs"]` makes the domain classifier push the same file into the `domain` bucket twice, and this code turns that into `Named(domain, [domain])` for both results. The CLI would then report a path as matching the same scope multiple times, which is not a real multi-scope match. Deduplicate `names` after sorting or classify each input occurrence independently.
